title: Missing ARM image for `make validatepr`
body:
The ARM build of the following image is missing for `make validatepr`:
- `quay.io/libpod/validatepr:latest`

comments.body:
You can find the source code here, to build your own `validatepr` image:

https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/contrib/validatepr

Well, I end up building it every time. Whether I'm experimenting with podman machine or cleaning up storage for testing purposes, I have to rebuild the image whenever I remove the machine or the image. I find it annoying that I have to think about this constantly. ~~It's not urgent, but given that we have other issues regarding missing image architectures, I think it would be a good idea to push this to quay.io as well.~~

There was a similar problem with the cache directories, always getting deleted

Now they are volumes, and can be either kept (or deleted like before) as you wish

There was a TODO, but no issue.

```
Makefile:# The image used below is generated manually from contrib/validatepr/Containerfile in this podman repo.  The builds are
Makefile-# not automated right now.  The hope is that eventually the quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman is multiarch and can replace this
Makefile-# image in the future.
```

Currently it is using `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora` instead of  `quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman`, though?

https://github.com/containers/automation_images

> There was a TODO, but no issue.
> 
> ```
> Makefile:# The image used below is generated manually from contrib/validatepr/Containerfile in this podman repo.  The builds are
> Makefile-# not automated right now.  The hope is that eventually the quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman is multiarch and can replace this
> Makefile-# image in the future.
> ```

Well, I know. Maybe it would be worth automating the build of that image including all architectures during Bugweek.

Then again you have to build Podman from source too, so it seems fair to have to test it from source :-)

I find it strange that there wasn't an arm64 version already, since it is mandatory and most are using Macs

I think building and pushing the ARM version of the `validatepr` image should be a priority right now, given the ContribFest at KubeCon.
cc @mheon

> I find it strange that there wasn't an arm64 version already, since it is mandatory and most are using Macs

There was an ARM version, but we had to update it and forgot about ARM.

But that was 3 months ago, and it is (assumed to) being run for _every_ PR?

* https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27965#issuecomment-3807008629

> But that was 3 months ago, and it is (assumed to) being run for every PR?

That image is for developer convenience. I just haven't had the time to report it until now. (I always forget to run make validatepr before pushing, and then CI yells at me.)
Image has been updated and pushed.
